equipment.name: Book in Draconic (From Eye of Watcher location)

traits: I grew up on the streets surrounded by similarly ill-fated castoffs, a few of them friends and a few of them rivals. I slept where I could and did odd jobs for food. At times, when the hunger became unbearable, I resorted to theft Still, I never lost my pride and never abandoned hope. Fate is not finished with me.
ideals:
Glory. I must earn glory in battle, for myself and my clan. (Any)
Aspiration. I'm going to prove that I'm worthy of a better life. (Any)
bonds:
I have a family, but I have no idea where they are. One day, I hope to see them again.
I owe my survival to another urchin who taught me to live on the streets
flaws:
I'm quick to assume that someone is trying to cheat me.
I don't always think and often start moving before I realise what I'm doing
backstory:
Ess was born and raised a Chieftain's daughter of one of the Aarakrocan Tribes in the Stormspire Isles. her village was one of the major tribes of the Isles, sitting atop a large mountain off the coast of Veldenar.

The Tribe of Sekar, her home, is a communal community where everyone is treated as family, and people are very close. Outside of chieftains meeting with other chieftains, there is little to no contact made with those outside of Sekar. 

Sekar, and all of the other islands in the Stormspire Islands have a close connection with an ancient deity known as Sylthira, the Storm Queen. She is worshipped all around the isles, with Statues and Monuments resembling her perceived bird-like appearance littered throughout the isles, with phrases and words of worship written in *Auran*. It is believed by the tribes people of the Stormspire Isles that Sylthira created the very skies and the seas that Ess and your people traverse every day, with each Storm coming as not a time of fear, but a time where Ess feel Sylthira's Prescence, knowing that she is there, watching over Ess all and keeping Ess all safe from harm.

Your father was traditional and proper. He despised her learning martial skills from her Grandmother, who hadnt been a chieftains daughter and was encouraged to pursue martial arts. She believed Ess should learn too. However, her father didnt like this and made efforts to stop this training whenever he could. This led to Ess learning the associated shadow arts to avoid him and those who hed send to disrupt trainings. Her father wanted her to follow in the footsteps of his ancestors, by being married off to another chieftain's son within the isles. At 13 years old, this caused Ess to run away from your tribe, to try and make a new life for herself where she could make her own decisions. However, when she left, she was caught up in a storm. Unable to combat the winds she ended up in the ocean where pirates found her. Upon landing at Nyleros they sold her to a pit fighter, where she has been fighting for her freedom since. 

Her martial skills she learnt from her Grandmother, who hadnt been a chieftains daughter and was encouraged to pursue martial arts. She believed Ess should learn too. However, her father didnt like this and made efforts to stop this training whenever he could. This led to Ess learning the associated shadow arts to avoid him and those who hed send to disrupt trainings.

After gaining quite the renown in the Nylerosi fight pits, becoming quite profitable for the Nobles who tookEss, a member of one of the Houses of Nyleros, Brauss Hawkspire (of House Hawkspire) wants to try and score a big win with the bounty that Ess've set. He ends up setting Ess up for failure, hiring some thugs to attack Ess before a big fight, where Brauss is sending in one of his Fighters in hopes to win the big bounty Ess've made in the pits. After barely surviving the encounter with Brauss' Hired Thugs, against all odds, Ess manage to defeat Brauss's chosen fighter, losing him quite a bit of money. Infuriated, Brauss puts a hit out on Ess, forcing Ess to try and flee to safety.

After running to the ports of Nyleros, towards the markets trying to blend in with all of the foreign folk in the area, Ess see a familiar sight; A Giant Airship from Meridan that Ess would have seen sail over **Sekar** many times in the past. Being well versed in sneaking around, silent as a shadow, Ess managed to sneak onto the Airship, fleeing Nyleros in fear for your life, not sure where this Airship will take Ess. After the Airship un-docks from Nyleros, it sails through the sky, towards the Flying City of Luminos.

feats.name: Lucky [Origin]
feats.desc: I gain a number of Luck Points equal to my Prof Bonus and can use them to give myself Advantage on a D20 Test, or to give a creature who rolls a d20 for an attack roll against me Disadvantage on that roll.

magicItems.name: Bag of Devouring
magicItems.desc: This bag is a feeding orifice for an extradimensional creature, which is closed if it is turned inside out. It devours all edible matter placed inside. Creatures partially inside get pulled in 50% of the time. Escaping (Str DC 15) or pulling another

magicItems.name: Gi of Shifting Seasons
magicItems.desc:
When found, this gi is gray with a large tree design at its bottom hem. When you attune to the gi, its color and tree design changes to resemble the current season: Winter (blue, leafless), Spring (green, pink blossoms), Summer (red, green leaves), or Autumn (Yellow, red leaves along the bottom hem). While the gi resembles a season, it gains the following respective benefits:

*Winter.* You are immune to the effects of extreme cold, and whenever you hit a creature with one of the attacks granted by your Flurry of Blows, that creature's speed is reduced by 5 feet (to a minimum speed of 15 feet) until the start of your next turn.

*Spring.* When the gi changes to this season, you gain 1d4 + 4 temporary hit points. In addition, when you use your Step of the Wind, your jump distance is tripled, instead of doubled, and you ignore the effects of difficult terrain until the end of your turn.

*Summer.* You are immune to the effects of extreme heat, and whenever you hit a target with an unarmed strike, the target takes an additional 2 fire damage. If you are outside during the day, the target takes an additional 3 fire damage, instead.

*Autumn.* You have advantage on Constitution saving throws. When you use your Patient Defense, creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they move within 5 feet of you until the start of your next turn.

You can change the gi's season to another one of your choice by spending 1 minute in silent meditation. Alternatively, whenever you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to spend 1 or more ki points, you can choose to shift the gi's season by one, moving in chronological order. When you do, you choose whether to gain the season's benefits before or after the action. For example, if the gi is in Autumn and you spend 1 ki point to use Flurry of Blows, the gi can immediately change to represent Winter and grant the season's benefits to your Flurry of Blows.

*The seasons change at their own pace: we cannot pretend to control nature's course. We must simply take time to enjoy the wisdom each season provides.*

magicItems.name: Fists of the Guiding Star
magicItems.desc:
Stars and celestial bodies glimmer through these hand wrappings. While wearing these wraps over your hands, you can use a bonus action to spend 1 focus point to cause small motes of starlight to guide a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, giving it advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of your next turn. If that creature's next attack hits, the attack deals extra radiant damage equal to your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1).

In addition, you can use an action to cast the *light* cantrip while wearing these wraps.

*When the stars align, we all prevail.*

magicItems.name: Eye of the Watcher
magicItems.desc:
While you are attuned to this item, you can cast *see invisibility* on yourself at will.

*Curse.* When you attune to the Eye of The Watcher, you are suddenly overcome with the urge to see what it sees. Make a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw. On a success, you're able to resist the enchantment and are aware of the curse's effects, but are not attuned to it. On a failure, you forcefully tear out one of your own Eyes, taking 4d10 necrotic damage, allowing the Eye of The Watcher to crawl into its place. If you are already missing an Eye, you are simply compelled to allow the Eye to crawl into the empty socket.

You can see normally while the Eye of The Watcher remains in your socket, but can choose to close your other Eye to see through its magically enhanced lens using a bonus action. Looking through the Eye grants you darkvision of up to 120 feet, and are able to peer through both magical and nonmagical darkness using it. The Eye moves slower than a normal Eye, leaving slight trails and motion blurs behind it as you look through it. While looking only through the Eye of The Watcher, you have disadvantage on any attack you make as well as ability checks that rely on depth perception. You can open your other Eye and regain your normal vision using another bonus action.

*The Watcher familiar.* The Eye is a gruesome familiar. As an action, you can command the Eye to crawl out of its socket and move independently of you. Use the abilities described in the *find familiar* spell when commanding the Eye of The Watcher. The Eye has five hit points, an AC of 12, a movement and climbing speed of 20 feet, does not require air to breathe, and has a +6 bonus to Dexterity (stealth) checks. If the Eye is slain or you dismiss it, it reappears inside your open Eye socket with a sickly sound. If it was slain, you cannot use its magical sight or command it as a familiar again until the following dusk.

Targeting the Eye with the *remove curse* spell or unattuning to it causes the Eye to pull itself out of the socket and crawl away. Slaying the Eye while it is unattuned to a creature destroys the item.

*The dark Eye swiveled, slightly. It was inhuman, to say the least, but its secrets were too vast to discredit.*

Eye Crawler — Tiny Aberration, Chaotic Neutral. Armor Class 12. Hit Points 2 (1d4). Speed 30 ft., Climb 30 ft. Initiative +1 (11). STR 3 (-4), DEX 13 (+1), CON 10 (+0), INT 6 (-2), WIS 11 (+0), CHA 5 (-3). Skills Stealth +6. Senses Darkvision 120 ft.

magicItems.name: Ring of Mind Shielding
magicItems.desc:
While wearing this ring, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type. Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it.
You can take a Magic action to cause the ring to become imperceptible until you take another Magic action to make it perceptible, until you remove the ring, or until you die.

If you die while wearing the ring, your soul enters it, unless it already houses a soul. You can remain in the ring or depart for the afterlife. As long as your soul is in the ring, you can telepathically communicate with any creature wearing it. A wearer can't prevent this telepathic communication.

Cursed
Once attuned to this ring, it cannot be removed unless the finger it was placed upon is removed. If the ring is removed in this way, the same user attempting to equip the ring once more will result in 6d12 psychic damage.

magicItems.name: Ring of Resistance
magicItems.desc: You have Resistance to one damage type while wearing this ring. The gem in the ring indicates the type of damage.
magicItems._baseDesc: You have Resistance to one damage type while wearing this ring. The gem in the ring indicates the type of damage.

name: Ess
